desc:
Silk strands dangle from the ceiling of a smooth-walled tunnel. Aged, grey husks
of butterflies hang from tightly-woven webs. One of these hapless creatures
seems to have escaped from one web only to get caught in another, telltale
strands trailing back to its penultimate snare on the opposite side of the
tunnel. The roof rises abruptly to the east, expanding into a vast and murky
cavern.
name: Sticky Tunnel

desc:
An enormous chamber cuts into the rock, its lofty ceiling soaring dozens of
yards above the rocky floor. Great spiralling webs interweave in irregular
patterns: old, dusty silk yields to new, sticky filaments. The floor is
blanketed in fallen strands that absorb the echoes one might otherwise expect in
a cavern of this size. A patch of fresh-looking webs stretch across a large hole
in the floor.
name: Vast, Webbed Cavern

desc:
A giant, sprawling web covers the ceiling of the eastern end of a spacious
chamber. Some of the strands hang to the floor, where they may clearly be seen
to be the thickness of a slender rope. Immense cocoons dangle from the web,
telling the harrowing tale of a creature capable of snagging such impressive
prey. Where the eastern wall of the chamber meets the ceiling, there is a dark
tunnel that leads upward.
name: Vast, Webbed Cavern

desc:
A rough tunnel zigs and zags as it slopes away from a vast chamber that lies
above it. Sticky spiders' weavings span the tunnel. The walls are rough and
gritty, made of grey-and-black speckled granite. The floor is covered in dusty
clumps of silk.
name: Webbed Tunnel

desc:
A rugged slab of rock juts from a mountainside to the north and hangs above a
charred valley far below. It slants gently downward as it separates from the
mountainside. Debris fallen from above litters the surface of the slab. Some
large boulders rest precariously at the edges. Broad, jagged cracks zigzag
inward from the sides of the slab. It is unclear how deep these cracks run and
how many more impacts this overhang can weather.
name: Precarious Overhang

desc:
A sheer rock wall to the west stretches far above the highest reach of decrepit
oaks that appear to claw at its face. The cliff face is riven with cracks, and
gaping holes show where pieces of rock have broken off. Numerous rocks and
boulders lie in the shadows of the trees.
name: Beneath Fractured Precipices

desc:
The corner of a plateau towers over this part of Mirkwood, its steep cliffs
running south and west. Rocks and small boulders appear to have tumbled down
from the sheer plateau wall, creating jagged points in the rock as they crashed
their way to the forest floor below. A broad shelf cuts westward, mediator
between the plateau and a valley to the north.
name: Beneath Fractured Precipices

desc:
Crumbling cliffs surround a dense copse of beeches. Shattered boulders mingled
with splintered branches indicate the slow retreat of the trees. A few shrubs
and stunted saplings have taken tentative root along unshaded ledges on the rock
walls.
name: Beneath Fractured Precipices

desc:
This broad cavern is littered with the bones of small creatures. Spatters of
blood stain the floor in dark brown patches. Quartz crystals bulge from the
ceiling, and shattered pieces glitter on the floor. The cave climbs gently
toward an opening to the east.
name: Spacious Den

desc:
A bed of leafy branches and tufts of grass sits in a dark corner of a cave,
hidden beneath a granite overhang. Several hard, crusty tubes of solidified
droppings rest a few yards away from the comfortable alcove. Piles of dark black
fur lie scattered across the floor.
name: Spacious Den

desc:
This underground passage weaves back and forth along natural veins in the rock.
It climbs from the clammy mist and rumbling din of a river to the stale, quiet
air of a den. The walls and floor are dark and smooth, reflecting no light. The
ceiling hides in a distant gloom.
name: Twisting Tunnel

desc:
A subterranean river of black water pours from a breach in the cavern wall to
the north. It flows westward, carving out a broad channel. To the east the
cavern floor climbs suddenly, and is mirrored by the cavern ceiling which rises
with it. Shelves of rock climb the steep face at the eastern end, meeting at
switchbacks which form a continuous path.
name: By a Subterranean River

desc:
A steady flow of black water pours from a gaping hole in the roof into a
shallow, murky pool. It flows thence into a broad, shallow channel that empties
into a subterranean river. The spray from the falling water chills the air as it
fills it with a fine mist.
name: By a Subterranean Cataract

desc:
A broad ledge overlooks a river that froths and bubbles as it flows through a
jumble of jagged boulders. The gurgling water sends a bitter mist wafting into
the air. The riverbanks are steep and composed of black and white marbled
granite.
name: By Subterranean Rapids

desc:
A murky river flows through the gaps in a line of rough boulders and broken
slabs. Slimy black moss thrives in this damp environment, blanketing the rocky
surfaces and making this crossing treacherous. A ledge climbs from the southern
bank of the river, rising as it follows the river eastward.
name: By a Subterranean River

desc:
A murky subterranean river cuts around a protruding granite rock formation. The
near bank is relatively flat and covered in small rocks and pebbles deposited by
the flowing water. Black moss covers the walls of this tunnel, thriving in the
wet environment. The damp air bears an acrid taste of noxious vapour.
name: By a Subterranean River

desc:
A tunnel leading from the west broadens into a large chamber facing a torrent of
pitch-black water that disappears around a sharp bend to the south. To the
north, the near bank is relatively flat, this in contrast to the steep wall that
runs along the distant bank. Tenuous webs stretch from the tunnel and across the
river into gloomy shadows on the other side.
name: By a Subterranean River

desc:
A broad, level shelf runs along the base of a high plateau to the south.
Opposite to the cliff there is a steep dropoff several dozen feet to a forested
valley floor. A web of cracks stretches across the southern cliff wall, which is
fractured in several places. Large slabs and boulders lie strewn across the
shelf. To the west, the ledge tapers to an end; there the plateau descends to
the valley in a single unbroken fall.
name: Beneath Fractured Precipices

desc:
Ripped webs dangle from the ceiling of this tunnel. Large rocks litter the
ground. Many of them bear stray strands of silk, some of which lead back to the
webs from which they were torn. A large mound of rubble sits against the
tapering eastern end of the tunnel; on the other end, the tunnel meets a sudden
end where it opens into the top of a large chamber.
name: Rubble-Clogged Tunnel

desc:
This chamber forms a rounded dome with smooth walls, except at its western end
where jagged scars show where rocks have been torn from the wall. The product of
this destruction is a massive pile that sits in front of the entrance to a
tunnel in the middle of the same wall. Several broken chisels and a large, crude
hammer are strewn around this entrance. The desiccated corpse of a spider rests
in the centre of the room, its head crushed by a massive boulder.
name: Marred Dome

desc:
The sound of rushing water reverberates throughout a recess at the eastern end
of a round dome. A black torrent gushes down a waterfall which intersects the
eastern end of this alcove as it cascades downward on its way to an unseen
destination far below. The chamber is spattered with spray from the protruding
rocks that interrupt the descending water. The floor is slippery and descends
away from the falls.
name: Subterranean Cataract

desc:
Sheer rock walls flank a narrow crevice bottom covered in a layer of shattered
rocks. The gap between the walls broadens considerably as the chasm expands
upward and eastward. A round hole in the southern wall leads to a remarkably
smooth-walled chamber.
name: Bottom of a Chasm

desc:
A great empty void extends upward from a rough cave floor covered in mounds of
guano. A swarming mass of black specks obscures the distant ceiling. Broken
rocks trail from the end of a slab that leans precariously against a ledge on
the northern wall.
name: Bottom of a Chasm

desc:
A narrow ledge runs along the edge of a deep chasm. Its surface is covered in
slimy bat droppings, making it very slippery. The ledge broadens to the west,
where it provides access to a narrow breach in the ceiling. To the east, this
shelf comes to an abrupt end where the slab that formed it has cracked, falling
to rest its distant end against the chasm floor below.
name: Slippery Ledge

desc:
A narrow ledge stands at the edge of a vast chamber which is dozens of yards
wide. This ledge continues around the chasm, descending gently as it runs
eastward along the northern end of this broad gap. A strong scent of fir drifts
in from a crack that leads upward.
name: Ledge Skirting a Chasm

desc:
Tall fir trees tower over this rugged plateau. Bone-white strands of gossamer
lichen drape from their branches and seem to hover in the breezes that whisk
along the rows of trunks. Enormous, step-like rock formations climb up to a
rugged shelf that skirts the terrain to the northeast. A gaping rent scars the
centre of the plateau, leading down to a vast chasm.
name: Fir-Clad Plateau

desc:
This uplift in the land gazes west over a broad and dark valley. Fir trees
stretch over the precipice, outspread branches casting shadows far below.
Strands of grey hairlike lichen droop from the trees. Farther away from the
cliff, this lichen is more closely cropped, a sign of grazing.
name: Fir-Clad Plateau

desc:
This region of the plateau is surprisingly devoid of trees. Mounds of boulders
form a half-ring around a dark, stagnant pool. The water is an opaque green, and
black lilies float silently on its surface. The rocks surrounding it are densely
carpeted in thick, yellow lichen; there are scattered bare patches where it has
been torn from the surface.
name: Fir-Clad Plateau

desc:
Short, steep cliffs rim this region of the plateau. Ranks of young fir trees
tufted in grey lichen stand in the lee of the overhanging rocks. Large, mossy
boulders gaze above the trees, casting shadows over the saplings. Yellow grass
grows between mounds of rock, partially obscured by the dry needles that litter
the ground. An impressive pile of recently-felled trees, their needles still
green, rests in front of a bulge on the southern cliff wall.
name: Fir-Clad Plateau

desc:
A massive slab juts from a high shelf to the north, creating a protected rock
shelter secure from the worst of the elements. Further to the east, the slab
leans down to merge with the plateau surface, forming a tunnel. A cavern mouth
opens northward and downward into the shelf, stalagmites and stalactites lining
an imposing maw.
name: Sheltered Cranny

desc:
A wall of solid rock lines a short, broad tunnel with openings to the east and
west. The only break in the wall is a yawning crevice that splits the floor in
half. The eastern end of the tunnel gazes down over the edge of a plateau,
providing a view of a valley stretching many miles to the east. A narrow sill
climbs northward along the sheer cliff face.
name: Sheltered Tunnel

desc:
Huge slabs of rock sit on the cliff edges, appearing as if only a slight push
would cause them to tumble down the precipice that stretches to the east and
south. The plateau is covered with barren trees and small grasses that somehow
cling to life, eeking out a meagre existance from the small deposits of soil
that made their way into cracks and fissures in the stone.
name: Rocky Plateau

desc:
The rocky plateau ends in sheer drops to the north and east. The cliff edges
have crumbled, probably due to rolling boulders that have disappeared from the
plateau. Nests and broken eggs can be found in gaps between the boulders and on
the the dry branches of withered stumpy trees.
name: Rocky Plateau

desc:
The shelf narrows here, flanked by steep drop-offs to north and south. Massive
slabs of rock protrude dozens of feet from the surface, and many hang
precariously over the cliffs to either side. The jagged outcrops are covered in
a thick layer of moss that makes them all the more hazardous. Various birds make
their homes above these precipices, safe in their isolation from the distant
forest floor.
name: Tall Shelf

desc:
This wide shelf of rock is concave, forming a depression towards its centre. A
stagnant pool of rainwater has gathered in the depression; it is covered in a
jet-black film of algae. Rocky outcrops on the edges of the shelf offer homes to
eagles and other wild fowl. Far below, a broad valley stretches to the north and
west.
name: Tall Shelf

desc:
Several rock pillars, each several feet wide, run through the centre of this
chamber. Piles of bedding lie on an elevated slab protruding from the eastern
wall where they are protected from the scattered puddles in the rest of the
cavern. A gash in the southern wall climbs up toward the surface.
name: Dank Cave

desc:
Crude garments and massive clubs are strewn about the floor of this cavern,
possessions of a clan of remarkably large humanoids. This cave must be an
uncomfortable abode for these unfortunates, as the height of the ceiling never
exceeds four yards.
name: Dank Cave

desc:
A slick slab juts into a descending deluge of water. A tunnel north of this slab
leads away from the torrent of water. It carries a fresh breeze that attempts to
sweep away the cold mist of the cascade. The waterfall roars as it reaches its
final destination somewhere in the gloom below.
name: Subterranean Cataract

desc:
Tall and venerable oak trees stand proud here in these woods, untouched by time
and the elements. Grey lichen hangs from the gnarled branches and moss covers
the rough bark, crawling its way upward to the few rays of light that manage to
pierce through the forest canopy. A musty odour rises up from the damp
vegetation on the forest floor.
name: Ancient Oaks

desc:
The tall, gnarly trees that populate this part of Mirkwood creak and groan even
when there is no wind to shake their crowns. A heavily intertwined canopy
consisting of vines, branches and leaves grows so densely that hardly any
natural light breaks through it. Gnarled black roots crawl across the forest
floor, oozing a stinky sap from sore-like wounds in their bark.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
Gnarled and squat oaks stand here with their branches drooping, as if protecting
the short, thorny brambles growing around their trunks. The musty smell of stale
air rises from the roots and the vegetation. Black and brown leaves cover the
forest floor, crunching noisily when stepped on.
name: Twisted Oaks

desc:
Travelling is quite difficult in the tangled vegetation of this flourishing
valley. Mountain sides can be seen through the dark green leaves that intertwine
to form a dense but not impenetrable canopy above. The overgrown floor of the
dell continues to the west, while travel in other directions is cut off by the
steep walls of the Emyn-nu-Fuin.
name: Overgrown Forest

desc:
A recent landslide from the shelf above this plateau has torn through wide ranks
of fir trees. Shattered trunks protrude from beneath the boulders that struck
them down. Fuzzy moss already grows on the mounds of rock, making a soft yet
slippery blanket.
name: Fir-Clad Plateau

desc:
Large boulders shelter a niche in the cliff wall that hides a secluded eyrie.
The niche offers a clear view of a valley full of blighted, dying trees. Damp,
acrid mist drifts from a tunnel that carves into the cliff to the south. A
distant roar of falling water emanates from the tunnel.
name: Isolated Nest
